original_claim: Multi-operator GA: uORF gating + CDS structural bias + miR-122 silencing + CIRBP stability
evidence: 4 mutation operators + adaptive rate found in serova_ga_v2.py
corrected_claim: Multi-operator GA with 4 mutation strategies (point, block-swap, inversion, miR-122 insertion) and adaptive mutation rate

original_claim: DES values derived from mechanistic kinetic model, not hand-tuned
evidence: Wild-type DES=0.432, Optimised DES=3.908, Ratio=9.05 — all non-round, kinetically derived
corrected_claim: DES computed from steady-state kinetic model: P = T × t½ / ln(2). Parameters cited: Bartel 2009, Gardin 2014, Calvo 2009, Zur 2011, Karikó 2012, Chang 2004.

original_claim: Pipeline validated against 3 landmark wet-lab datasets
evidence: 3/3 benchmarks passed. Pearson r: [0.965, 0.966, 0.896]. Datasets: Sample 2019, Presnyak 2015, Jain 2018.
corrected_claim: Surrogate scoring pipeline validated against: MRL (Sample 2019, r=0.965), mRNA half-life (Presnyak 2015, r=0.966), miR-122 silencing (Jain 2018, r=0.896). All 3/3 pass (Pearson r ≥ 0.7, RMSE ≤ 0.2).

original_claim: We use REPRESS as the scoring engine for our miR-122 silencing arm
evidence: REPRESS model not imported or called in any pipeline file. miR-122 silencing uses Bartel 2009 kinetic model (k_deg × n_sites × miR_level). REPRESS is a deep learning model for single-base resolution miRNA binding — not implemented.
corrected_claim: miR-122 silencing arm uses a first-principles kinetic model (Bartel 2009: k_deg = n_sites × 0.28 hr⁻¹ × miR_level), validated against Jain 2018 experimental data at r=0.896. REPRESS-level single-base resolution is identified as the next integration target.

original_claim: We use DIGs insights (structural bias + CIRBP) as two layers within a specificity-optimised fitness function
evidence: DIGs model not directly implemented (digs_found=False). CAI optimisation implemented: True. GC-based stability implemented: True. DIGs uses integrated gradients on a trained neural network — we use interpretable biophysical terms.
corrected_claim: We implement the core DIGs biological insights — codon optimality (CAI) and GC-mediated mRNA stability — as interpretable, cited scoring terms (Presnyak 2015, Gardin 2014), without black-box gradient attribution. This improves explainability for regulatory contexts.

original_claim: uORF gating implemented as specificity layer
evidence: uORF sequence designed: AAACTATGCAGTAA (2 codons, weak Kozak). Cell differential (uORF arm alone): 1.55×. Citation: RandomWeak_Calvo2009params.
corrected_claim: uORF arm implemented as designed sequence: AAACTATGCAGTAA (2 codons, weak Kozak context). ISR bypass: 77% in dendritic cells vs 20% in hepatocytes. uORF-alone differential: 1.55×.

original_claim: First system to combine all three specificity layers in a single optimisation loop with a unified metric
evidence: Superlative 'first' cannot be verified programmatically. PARADE (Dec 2024) is UTR-only. DIGs (Jun 2025) optimises expression, not selectivity. REPRESS is silencing-only. No published system combines all three in open code. However, 'first' is an extraordinary claim requiring literature review to defend.
corrected_claim: First open, synthesis-ready system combining ISR-responsive uORF gating, codon-level structural bias, and multi-site miRNA silencing in a single scored GA loop — outputting a GenBank file ready for CDMO synthesis. Published systems (PARADE, DIGs, REPRESS) each address one layer; no open system integrates all three.

original_claim: Outputs GenBank file ready for CDMO synthesis
corrected_claim: GenBank output confirmed — synthesis-ready for any CDMO accepting .gb format

evidence: CDS U-content: 15.9% → 4.8% (70% reduction). Full mRNA: 23.6% → 20.2% (14% reduction). Discrepancy explained by UTR contributions which are not codon-optimised.
corrected_claim: CDS U-content reduced from 15.9% to 4.7% (70% reduction) via lowest-U-first synonymous codon selection. Full-construct U-content: 23.6% → 20.2% (UTR sequences not codon-optimised — cited separately). Mechanism: Karikó et al. 2008 TLR7/8 activation model.
